title: Docs: --mount should have a dedicated section describing each type
body:
The current `--mount` documentation skips straight from the brief statement overview for `--mount` itself and the listing of all supported types straight into the description for the options for each type (i.e. no description for the types themselves).

It would be beneficial to have a section that describes the function of each individual mount type. Visually, my request is:

```
--mount=type=TYPE,TYPE-SPECIFIC-OPTION[,…]

Attach a filesystem mount to the container.

Current supported mount TYPEs are artifact, bind, devpts, glob, image, ramfs, tmpfs and volume.

<PARAGRAPH(S) DESCRIBING WHAT EACH TYPE DOES HERE>

Options common to all mount types:

<...>
```

For reference, [here is the Docker section](https://docs.docker.com/engine/storage/) that provides an overview for each of their supported mount types.

On top of general overviews of each type, it would be nice to have example use cases for each (i.e. "When to use `TYPE` mount"). Docker includes such sections for both its [`volume`](https://docs.docker.com/engine/storage/volumes/#when-to-use-volumes) and [`bind`](https://docs.docker.com/engine/storage/bind-mounts/#when-to-use-bind-mounts) mount options.

comments.body:
One documentation design idea could be to generate out markdown sources for the mount options from a JSON file.
See https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24249#issuecomment-2808498141
Interested in opening a PR to make this change?

> Interested in opening a PR to make this change?

I will focus on https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24249. This issue (#25888) is more about adding more information. In the other issue  (#24249) I started thinking about generating out mount option documentation from a script.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
